title: Multispectral Imaging (Landsat/Sentinel-2)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Multispectral Imaging (Landsat/Sentinel-2): multispectral filter produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG L.band_filter -> S.scan.pushbroom -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D spectral image. Difficulty tier delta=1 with effective condition number kappa_eff~5; calibration-level mismatch (atmospheric_correction_error, bidirectional_reflectance, cloud_contamination) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y_b(x,y) = integral R(x,y,lambda) * filter_b(lambda) * atm_b dlambda + path_radiance_b + n
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D spectral image is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 5); atmospheric_correction_error dominates the stability cliff; bidirectional_reflectance and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [DOS, Sen2Cor] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [MSI-UNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |atmospheric_correction_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_spectral_bins * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
